Description: The primary open-source web framework for building modern web apps and APIs in .NET.
Recommendation: Essential. This is the foundation for your Web API and hosting your Angular/React/Bootstrap frontend. It is high-performance and fully supported by Microsoft.

Description: The official modern object-database mapper for .NET. Supports LINQ queries, change tracking, updates, and schema migrations.
Recommendation: Use this as your primary ORM for PostgreSQL (via Npgsql). It is significantly faster than older versions and handles complex relationships required in B2B SaaS tenancy models perfectly.

Description: The .NET data provider for PostgreSQL. It allows you to connect and interact with PostgreSQL server.
Recommendation: Required. This is the underlying driver that EF Core will use to talk to your PostgreSQL database. It supports advanced Postgres features like JSONB and arrays out of the box.

Description: High-performance general purpose Redis client for .NET.
Recommendation: Use for your caching layer. It handles high throughput efficiently. Wrap this with a caching service or use IDistributedCache in ASP.NET Core for simpler scenarios.

Description: The built-in, high-performance, low-allocation JSON library for .NET.
Recommendation: Use this instead of Newtonsoft.Json. It is the default in .NET Core, much faster, and strictly standards-compliant. Essential for your API payloads and storing JSONB in Postgres.

Description: A diagnostic logging library for .NET applications. It emphasizes structured logging (logging data, not just text).
Recommendation: Must-have. Configure it to sink to Elasticsearch (or files/console during dev). Structured logging is vital for debugging SaaS issues where you need to filter logs by TenantId or UserId.

Description: A free, open-source distributed application framework for .NET. It abstracts the message bus (RabbitMQ, Azure Service Bus, Amazon SQS).
Recommendation: Essential for your Pub/Sub requirement. It simplifies complex messaging patterns (Request/Response, Sagas, Retry policies) and ensures your async messaging is robust and decoupled.

Description: A .NET workflow engine that allows you to execute workflows defined in code or via a visual designer (similar to ActivePieces/Zapier but embedded).
Recommendation: Perfect fit for your "orchestration/ActivePieces" requirement. Use it to build user-definable workflows (e.g., "If lead created, send email") directly inside your SaaS.

Description: A popular .NET library for building strongly-typed validation rules.
Recommendation: Use this to validate incoming API requests (DTOs). It separates validation logic from your business logic and controllers, keeping your code clean and testable.

Description: The automatic type-safe REST library for .NET. It turns your REST API definition into a live interface.
Recommendation: Use this for server-to-server communication (e.g., if your backend needs to call other microservices or external APIs). It is cleaner than manually using HttpClient.

Description: A resilience and transient-fault-handling library that allows developers to express policies such as Retry, Circuit Breaker, Timeout, and Fallback.
Recommendation: Wrap your external API calls (Redis, Elasticsearch, 3rd party APIs) with Polly policies to prevent your SaaS from crashing when a dependency blips.

Description: An easy way to perform background processing in .NET and .NET Core applications. No Windows Service or separate process required.
Recommendation: Use for background jobs that are not message-based (e.g., nightly billing reports, database cleanup). It has a built-in dashboard which is excellent for B2B admins to monitor job status.

Description: The official strongly typed client for Elasticsearch.
Recommendation: Use for your search requirements. It maps .NET objects to Elasticsearch documents. Ensure you use the v8+ client for modern features and better performance.

Description: Simple, unambitious mediator implementation in .NET. Supports request/response, commands, queries, notifications, and events.
Recommendation: Highly recommended to decouple your API Controllers from business logic. It pairs perfectly with CQRS patterns often used in scalable SaaS architectures.

Description: A fast, code-generation based object mapper (alternative to AutoMapper).
Recommendation: Use for mapping your Database Entities to API DTOs. It is faster and more memory-efficient than AutoMapper, which is crucial for high-load SaaS applications.

Description: A date and time handling library for .NET (spiritual successor to Joda Time in Java).
Recommendation: Use this if your SaaS deals with complex time zones or scheduling (e.g., booking systems). For simple "CreatedDate" timestamps, standard .NET DateTimeOffset is sufficient, but NodaTime prevents time-zone bugs.

Description: The premier unit testing framework for .NET, written by the original inventor of NUnit.
Recommendation: Use for all your backend testing. It is modern, opinionated, and works seamlessly with .NET Core parallel test execution.

Description: A set of .NET extension methods that allow you to more naturally specify the expected outcome of a TDD or BDD-style unit test.
Recommendation: Use this in conjunction with xUnit to make your test code readable and expressive (e.g., result.Should().Be(5)).

Description: Provides methods and algorithms for numerical computations in science, engineering and every day use.
Recommendation: Use this if you need the "Numpy" equivalent for .NET. It handles matrices, linear algebra, and statistics efficiently for backend calculations.

Description: An open source and cross-platform machine learning framework for .NET.
Recommendation: Use this if you need "PyTorch" style capabilities natively in C#. It allows you to train models or consume ONNX models (exported from PyTorch/TensorFlow) directly in your .NET app.

Description: Generates beautiful API documentation (Swagger/OpenAPI) directly from your code and XML comments.
Recommendation: Mandatory. This generates the swagger.json that your frontend (Typescript) developers can use to auto-generate their API clients, ensuring frontend/backend sync.
